item.title: Hugging Face and Cerebras bring Gemma 4 to real-time voice AI
item.description: Focus: Hugging Face, Cerebras has a new AI-related update around 'Hugging Face and Cerebras bring Gemma 4 to real-time voice AI', relevant to product, industry or technical direction.

item.title: NVIDIA and Partners Build in America, for America
item.description: Focus: NVIDIA and its partners are investing in American manufacturing, supply chains, energy grids and skilled workforces so the U.S. can produce the infrastructure needed for better healthcare, breakthrough scientific discovery, stronger industrial productivity and global technology leadership.

item.title: The Winning Essays for the Big Questions About AI
item.description: Focus: Abolishing pandemics/ Getting out of the way of AI automation/ Learning from Honk Kong MTR's business model

item.title: How Cursor deploys AI inside the enterprise
item.description: Focus: Cursor's Pauline Brunet explains how her team of Forward Deployed Engineers help organizations implement agents — essentially setting up software factories.

item.title: 🔬 The Coolest Diffusion Research Isn't in LLMs — Evan Feinberg & Sergey Edunov, Genesis Molecular AI
item.description: Focus: Why the Llama lead left Meta for drug discovery, PEARL's zero-shot OpenBind win, and what becomes possible when co-folding finally crosses the accuracy threshold.

item.title: Warp CEO Zach Lloyd on why software factories are the next phase of coding
item.description: Focus: Warp's founder thinks every major software project will soon run on an automated factory. He discusses why and how engineers should prepare for this shift.

item.title: AIEWF Daily Dispatch: Loops, Software Factories & Forward Deployed Engineers
item.description: Focus: On Tuesday at the AI Engineer World's Fair, there was a lot of talk about loops, agent engineering, and the emergence of software factories. Also a hot topic: open models.

item.title: Forward Deployed Engineers and the future of software engineering
item.description: Focus: Sierra's Natalie Meurer on why product engineers and forward deployed engineers are starting to converge.

item.title: Ahmad Osman on why local AI is catching up
item.description: Focus: After two packed AIEWF workshops, Ahmad Osman makes the case that local AI is catching up fast — from laptops and phones to enterprise-grade infrastructure.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 access restored on AI Gateway
item.description: Focus: Access to Claude Fable 5, the Mythos-class model, has now been restored on AI Gateway following the US Government's decision to lift the export controls. Fable 5 is the same model that was available between June 9 and June 12. What has changed is the safety classifiers, which are now updated

item.title: Into the Omniverse: Three Workflows for Improving Vision AI Agent Accuracy With Synthetic Data and Fine-Tuning
item.description: Focus: Editor’s note: This post is part of Into the Omniverse, a series focused on how developers, 3D practitioners, and enterprises can transform their workflows using the latest advances in OpenUSD and NVIDIA Omniverse. Vision AI agents are becoming a practical way to automatically turn video data

item.title: Announcing our $800M Series C to accelerate the shift to open-source AI
item.description: Focus: We raised $800M to accelerate the shift to open-source AI. Here's why the economics of closed models don't scale, and what we're building next.

item.title: Secure internal communication between services
item.description: Focus: Service Bindings make it easy for one Vercel service to securely call another within the same deployment. When a service declares a binding for another service, Vercel automatically injects the configured environment variable. That means user code can fetch that URL normally, while Vercel han

item.title: Enforce consistent code for agents and humans with konsistent
item.description: Focus: konsistent is now open source. konsistent is a CLI linter for TypeScript codebases that enforces structural conventions, giving agents and humans the consistent context they need to implement features correctly. Deterministic, fast, and covers structural patterns that TypeScript and ESLint do

item.title: Dry-run deployments with Vercel CLI
item.description: Focus: You can now preview the framework preset and files that Vercel CLI includes in a deployment before creating one. Run vercel deploy --dry from a linked project: For automation or further inspection, return the complete file manifest as JSON: JSON output includes the detected framework, include

item.title: Vercel Security Dashboard is in private beta
item.description: Focus: The Vercel Security Dashboard is now in private beta. It aggregates the security posture of every account and project on Vercel. As teams grow and coding agents make it easy to spin up new projects, small misconfigurations can add up quietly and quickly. Team members without 2FA, publicly acc

item.title: Resend joins the Vercel Marketplace
item.description: Focus: Resend is now available on the Vercel Marketplace , allowing Vercel teams to send emails from their applications without managing any infrastructure. This integration helps developers start sending messages within minutes, build emails as React components, and track delivery events in real ti
